wc -w /testbed/*.txt | grep -v total | awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum/NR}'

raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to compute mean average of word count of *.txt files in /testbed. Use wc -w and average. Command: awk. For example: `awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum/NR}' <(wc -w /testbed/*.txt | grep -v total)`. But wc -w outputs count and filename. We need to sum counts. Use `wc -w /testbed/*.txt | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1} END{print sum/NR-1}'` but better: `wc -w /testbed/*.txt | grep -v total | awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum/NR}'`. Provide single command. Let's output.
